title: CPP and EI rulings - Canada.ca
content:
How to get a Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and employment insurance (EI) ruling to determine if a worker is an employee or self-employed, whether an employment or earnings are pensionable, insurable, or both, how to get a certificate of coverage for CPP purposes for workers working abroad, and other matters relating to the CPP and EI.
- Not dealing at arm’s length for purposes of the EI Act
- Employment outside Canada
- Employer restructuring / Succession of employers
- Canada Pension Plan retirement pension
- Employment Insurance benefits

## Services and information

### Employment status: Employee or self-employed
Determine a worker’s employment status and if the employment is pensionable, insurable, or both

### Request a CPP/EI ruling
How and when to request a ruling, ruling process, implications of a ruling, and what to do if you disagree with a ruling

### If you receive a CPP/EI ruling
What to know if you have received a ruling
Whether an employment is pensionable or insurable, specific industries, special employment situations

### International social security agreements and the CPP
CPP contributions and foreign pension exemptions when working abroad, and certificates of coverage
Use the new online learning tool to learn about taxes and the differences between being an employee and a self-employed individual.
Learn more about what is the CPP enhancement
- Canada Revenue Agency

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
